what_to_implement.module_signature_1: compute_target_weights(signal_scores: dict, daily_panel: pd.DataFrame, as_of: date, sector_mapping: dict, config: dict) -> dict[ts_code, float]

sizing_logic.raw_target_per_position: min(0.10, 0.02 * target_vol / stock_atr_vol) where target_vol=0.20 portfolio annualized vol, stock_atr_vol = ATR(14)/close * sqrt(252)
sizing_logic.renormalize: If sum > 0.95, scale down proportionally. If single-sector > 30%, trim within sector to top scorers.

what_to_implement.module_signature_2: evaluate_exits(positions: dict, daily_panel: pd.DataFrame, as_of: date, signal_scores: dict) -> dict[ts_code, str]

exit_rules.take_profit_half: position.mtm_pct ≥ +0.10 from entry; sell half AND raise trailing to entry price

circuit_breakers.drawdown_15pct: If portfolio_dd ≤ -0.15: block_new_positions + reduce gross to 0.60
circuit_breakers.drawdown_25pct: If portfolio_dd ≤ -0.25: flat_all + wait 5 trading days
circuit_breakers.high_vol_regime: If realized_vol_60d > 0.30 annualized: reduce gross to 0.50

what_to_implement.PIT_discipline: All exit evaluations use only data with trade_date ≤ as_of.

selftest_requirements: Sizing: 8 stocks with various ATR — high-ATR get smaller weights, low-ATR get larger; sum ≤ 0.95
Sector cap: feed 5 stocks all in same sector → should cap sector total at 30%
Exit: position with mtm -10% triggers hard_stop; position with peak +12% and current +6% triggers trailing
Circuit breaker: DD -16% triggers block_new + scale_to_60
